title: French bitcoin evangelical Eric Larchevêque wants his right-wing voice to be part of public debate
subhed: Known to the public as a judge on a television show, the French bitcoin pioneer wants to win over the masses to the cryptocurrencies that made him wealthy. With the 2027 presidential election approaching, this libertarian has embraced increasingly radical ideas.

Iran missile strikes wound over 100 in two southern Israel towns
subhed: Iran retaliated after its main nuclear enrichment site was hit, striking two southern Israeli cities, Dimona and Arad, late Saturday evening. It is the first time that Israel's nuclear research center has been targeted in the fighting.

title: The UAE is reorganizing its arms supply network for Sudanese paramilitaries
subhed: Abu Dhabi is seeking to develop new logistical routes from Sudan's neighboring countries, such as Ethiopia and the Central African Republic, to support the Rapid Support Forces.

title: The truffle epidemic: 'I'm worried we'll soon have truffle shampoo'
subhed: The rise – and imminent fall – of the truffle, a once rare luxury ingredient whose proliferation is now reaching saturation point.

title: International aid convoy reaches Cuba amid humanitarian crisis
subhed: More than 500 worldwide activists have arrived in Havana to deliver to the Cuban authorities roughly 20 metric tons of food supplies, medical equipment.

subhed: Since stripping Senegal of the continental championship title, ruling the team had 'forfeited,' the Confédération Africaine de Football (CAF) has faced intense criticism over its ability to manage competitions.

title: With conviction of jihadist Sabri Essid, French judiciary recognizes Yazidi genocide for first time
subhed: By sentencing Essid to life imprisonment for 'genocide' on Friday, the Paris court followed the example set by Germany, Sweden and Belgium. The Islamic State’s plan to 'destroy' this minority, which has never been the subject of an international investigation, is now recognized by several European courts.

title: Catherine MacGregor, Engie CEO, on gas prices: 'There are many differences compared to 2022'
subhed: In an interview with Le Monde, the head of the French energy group discusses the repercussions of the war in the Middle East. She believes that, thanks to nuclear and renewable energy, France is less exposed than other countries.
